name: Charles McPherson
abstract: Charles McPherson (born July 24, 1939) is an American jazz alto saxophonist born in Joplin, Missouri, United States; raised in Detroit, Michigan; and now lives in San Diego, California. He worked intermittently with Charles Mingus from 1960 to 1974, and as a performer leading his own groups.

interviews.title: Charles McPherson interviewed by Monk Rowe, San Diego, California, February 12, 1998 [interview 1 of 2]

about_quotes.speaker: Jimmy Owens
about_quotes.evidence: In the station wagon... was me, Marcus Belgrave, and Howard Johnson... Charles McPherson alto saxophone. So, it was all brass plus saxophone, and Danny Richmond and Mingus.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jimmy Owens — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Barry Harris
about_quotes.evidence: Charles McPherson, he was about, he might have been fifteen when he started with me... I insisted that, “you’re gonna get all A’s.” ... So everything I said, he would do.
about_quotes.doc_title: Barry Harris — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Howard Johnson
about_quotes.evidence: Mingus was there with a quintet with Charles McPherson... So I had met Jaki... And I went to see Mingus... I was trying to sit in the back... Mingus had just finished his 'Mingus at Monterey' recording... So it was a great record, and when he came back to New York he wanted to play that music... wit
about_quotes.doc_title: Howard Johnson interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 14, 2019

about_quotes.speaker: Michael Weiss
about_quotes.evidence: [(note)] Michael Weiss has made a significant contribution to jazz as a pianist, composer, and educator. In addition to leading his own ensembles, Michael has played with numerous jazz luminaries, including Johnny Griffin, Art Farmer, and Charles McPherson.
about_quotes.doc_title: Michael Weiss interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, December 22, 2021

about_quotes.speaker: Tim Ries
about_quotes.evidence: Charles McPherson and all those amazing, you know Elvin and all those Detroit — Joe © Fillius Jazz Archive -13- Henderson, Pepper Adams — all the people who came through Detroit
about_quotes.doc_title: Tim Ries interviewed by Monk Rowe, Utica, New York, March 19, 2022

about_quotes.speaker: Kristen Korb
about_quotes.evidence: Oh yeah, James Moody, Charles McPherson, Mundell Lowe, Barney Kessel lives there... But yeah, every once in a while he’ll come to one of my gigs and hang out.
about_quotes.doc_title: Kristen Korb interviewed by Monk Rowe, Scottsdale, Arizona, April 16, 2000
